name: Oregon
tooltip: Oregon — Cat 4: No codified statutory right of publicity. Common-law misappropriation may apply.
rightOfPublicity: Common law only. Oregon has not enacted a codified general right-of-publicity statute. Common-law misappropriation may apply — elements vary, but generally: defendant appropriated plaintiff's identity for commercial value, plaintiff is identifiable, defendant received a benefit. Consult an attorney for state-specific application.
postMortem: No codified post-mortem term. Descendibility uncertain at common law.
aiCoverage: ORS 260.345 (SB 1571, 2024, election synthetic media). ORS 163.472 (HB 2299, 2025 Or. Laws ch. 116, eff. Jan. 1, 2026).
remedy: Common-law misappropriation suit. Federal claims available regardless: Lanham Act § 43(a) false endorsement, defamation, and federal NCII statutes apply nationwide.
whatsInteresting: Oregon hasn't built statutory likeness protection. AI-specific statutes exist for narrow areas (election deepfakes, NCII, etc.) but the general right-of-publicity base is common law only.
primarySources: Common-law cases vary by state; consult an attorney licensed in Oregon.
Federal statutes and any state AI-specific statutes listed above.
